text: Generating well-calibrated probabilistic predictions is essential for effective decision-making in artificial intelligence, yet many popular supervised learning algorithms produce outputs that are poorly calibrated. This paper introduces Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ), a novel non-parametric post-processing method designed to enhance the calibration of binary classification models. BBQ extends traditional histogram binning by considering a space of multiple equal-frequency binning models and combining their outputs through Bayesian model averaging. This strategy mitigates common limitations such as the selection of a fixed number of bins and sensitivity to data distribution. We evaluate the proposed method on both simulated and real-world benchmark datasets, comparing its performance against standard techniques like Platt scaling and isotonic regression. Our empirical results demonstrate that BBQ significantly improves calibration—measured by Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and Maximum Calibration Error (MCE)—while maintaining or enhancing the discriminative power of the underlying classifier. The method is computationally efficient and can be easily integrated with any binary classification framework.
